author: Dr. Michael Torres
content:
Telephone encounter with patient Robert Morrison regarding recent lab results and medication management.

REASON FOR CALL: Patient called requesting review of lab results drawn 8/1/2025 at outpatient lab. Also requesting refill of sodium bicarbonate which pharmacy says needs new prescription.

LAB RESULTS REVIEWED:
- BMP: Na 138, K 4.6, Cl 102, HCO3 21 (improved from 19 on prior draw — bicarb supplementation helping), BUN 42, Cr 2.08 (slight uptick from 1.95 baseline, but within expected fluctuation), glucose 162
- Calculated eGFR: 32 mL/min (was 35 last check — gradual expected decline)
- Urine albumin/creatinine ratio: 380 mg/g (stable, previously 350-400 range)
- Phosphorus: 4.8 (upper normal, monitoring)
- Intact PTH: 88 pg/mL (mildly elevated, consistent with CKD-MBD)
- A1c: 7.4% (improved from 7.6% — diabetes management improving)

Discussed results with patient. He was concerned about the creatinine increase — reassured him that Cr 2.08 is within his expected range and not a significant change from 1.95. Explained that CKD Stage 3b does involve gradual decline but his is progressing slowly. The improvement in bicarbonate from 19 to 21 is encouraging and confirms the sodium bicarbonate is working.

Reviewed that his albumin/creatinine ratio remains stable — important to continue Entresto as it provides renal protection in addition to cardiac benefits. Reminded him to avoid NSAIDs (he confirmed his wife asked about this recently and they understand).

PTH mildly elevated at 88 — not yet at threshold for intervention but will continue to monitor. Phosphorus still in normal range. Will consider phosphate binder if phosphorus rises above 5.5 or PTH continues to climb.

MEDICATION MANAGEMENT:
- Sodium bicarbonate 650mg BID — sent new prescription to CVS (90-day supply). Confirmed he has been taking consistently with meals.
- Reviewed that empagliflozin (started May 2025) is going well — patient reports no UTI symptoms, no genital infections, staying well hydrated. Confirmed he is checking blood sugars regularly.
- Reminded patient about potassium dietary restrictions given K 4.6 on Entresto + spironolactone.

ADDITIONAL DISCUSSION: Patient asked about whether his kidney disease will eventually require dialysis. Explained that at current rate of decline (~2-3 mL/min/year eGFR loss), dialysis is not imminent but is a possibility in the coming years. Recommended continuing to optimize blood pressure, diabetes control, and medication adherence to slow progression. Discussed that empagliflozin has shown renal protective benefits in clinical trials which is part of why it was added.

Patient asked about kidney-friendly diet — confirmed he is working with dietitian (saw Amanda Rodriguez, RD in March 2024) and should continue those recommendations. Low sodium, moderate protein (0.8 g/kg/day), potassium restriction, phosphorus awareness.

PLAN:
1. Sodium bicarbonate refill sent to CVS.
2. Continue current nephrology medication regimen.
3. Recheck BMP, phosphorus, PTH in 3 months (November 2025).
4. Next in-person nephrology visit scheduled for October 2025.
5. Continue empagliflozin — monitoring renal function closely with new agent on board.
6. If eGFR drops below 25, will discuss nephrology referral pathway for transplant evaluation and dialysis access planning.

Total call duration: 18 minutes.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Michael Torres
Date: 8/6/2025
